page: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 8 physical cores and 16 virtual cores (vCPUs) via hyperthreading. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AVX-512 (including AVX-512 VNNI), AES, VAES, and SHA-NI cryptographic extensions.

This server is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It includes a bundled local storage capacity of 1229 GB and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The high memory density relative to compute resources positions this instance for memory-intensive workloads, while the integrated local disk provides high-capacity temporary storage. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications by avoiding the need to provision excess compute cores solely to acquire sufficient memory capacity.

In benchmark testing, the E32-16ds_v5 demonstrated a Passmark CPU score of 21,361.52 and a Passmark memory score of 2,658.98. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 834.58 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 101.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations reached 8,047.55 on the Passmark database test, and Redis SET operations achieved 15.94 million operations per second. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server processed 1,082.45 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model, dropping to 35.05 tokens per second on a 7B parameter model. These metrics indicate suitability for memory-constrained databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 256.0 GB of RAM (16.0 GB per vCPU), and 1229 GB of bundled local storage. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. In performance testing, it delivered a Passmark CPU score of 21,361.52, a memory score of 2,658.98, and processed 15.94 million Redis SET operations per second. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity, this server is designed for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory overhead relative to compute capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio, integrated local storage, and advanced cryptographic acceleration for database workloads.

256.0 GB of RAM providing 16.0 GB per vCPU
